items.title: Backtest strategy framework beyond buy_and_hold
items.reason: The backtest engine is functional but hardcodes buy_and_hold. A pluggable strategy framework exists in strategies/ but is not wired. This limits paper-mode demonstration value.
items.acceptance_criteria: At least one additional strategy (e.g., simple moving-average crossover) is wired and tested in the backtest engine without changing the CLI surface.
items.non_goals: Does not add live trading strategies. Does not change backtest CLI args. Does not add external data feeds.
items.reviewer_notes: Safe local-only change. No broker or provider execution involved.

items.title: Clarify provider execution boundary in public docs
items.reason: The inventory marks provider execution as docs_only because there is no runtime API lock. The README and docs should be crystal clear that provider calls are governed by discipline gates and risk manager (which block resulting orders), not a runtime network block.
items.acceptance_criteria: README and product-capability-inventory.md explicitly state that provider execution locking is an artifact/policy boundary, not a runtime network block, and that risk manager prevents order submission.
items.non_goals: Does not add a runtime network lock on provider calls. Does not change provider adapter code.
items.reviewer_notes: Docs-only change. Critical for honest public messaging.

items.title: Skill miner static placeholder
items.reason: Skill lifecycle plumbing is complete, but skill_miner.py returns a static template with no LLM integration. This is a known limitation that does not block release.
items.acceptance_criteria: Skill miner produces meaningful proposed skills from trade journal entries, or docs clearly label it as a planned future enhancement.
items.non_goals: Does not require provider execution. Does not change the skills approval/archive flow.
items.reviewer_notes: Low priority. The lifecycle plumbing is the valuable part for v0.5.8.

items.title: Self-improvement loop AI-driven extraction
items.reason: The learning loop orchestration exists but reflections, nudges, and skill mining are static templates. Real AI-driven extraction requires careful safety review and is not a v0.5.8 blocker.
items.acceptance_criteria: Reflection generation and nudges use LLM calls with deterministic safety guardrails, or remain static with clear docs.
items.non_goals: Does not enable autonomous trading decisions. Does not bypass discipline gates.
items.reviewer_notes: Defer until post-v0.5.8. Static templates are acceptable for this release.

items.title: Daily and weekly report generators
items.reason: Report generators are stubs. Real content already comes from the routine engine. Making the stubs useful or documenting them as stubs is worthwhile polish.
items.acceptance_criteria: Report stubs either generate useful Markdown from routine output, or docs clearly state they are placeholders.
items.non_goals: Does not add new report types. Does not change the routine engine.
items.reviewer_notes: Low priority. Routine engine already produces the meaningful content.

items.title: Complete Binance broker adapter and enable CCXT/IBKR
items.reason: Binance is partial, CCXT is disabled, and IBKR is a placeholder. Alpaca is the only fully implemented live broker. Expanding broker support requires extensive safety review and is not a v0.5.8 blocker.
items.acceptance_criteria: Binance flatten_all and cancel_order are implemented. CCXT adapter is functional. IBKR stub is replaced with real adapter. All pass the same safety gates as Alpaca.
items.non_goals: Does not enable live submit by default. Does not change BrokerResolver gating logic.
items.reviewer_notes: Touches protected broker boundaries. Requires safety review. Not for v0.5.8.

items.title: Slack notifications integration
items.reason: Only a no-op stub exists. ClickUp and Telegram gateway are already implemented. Slack is not a v0.5.8 blocker.
items.acceptance_criteria: Slack notifier sends messages via webhook with secret redaction and rate limiting.
items.non_goals: Does not add Slack slash commands. Does not change existing notifiers.
items.reviewer_notes: Nice to have. Not critical for v0.5.8.

items.title: README docs do not overclaim production or live trading readiness
items.reason: Before v0.5.8, every public-facing doc must be audited to ensure no sentence can be read as implying Atlas is production-ready for unattended or real-money trading.
items.acceptance_criteria: scripts/check_forbidden_claims.py passes. A maintainer has read README.md, docs/product-capability-inventory.md, and docs/controlled-reviewer-outreach.md and confirmed no overclaim.
items.non_goals: Does not rewrite all docs. Only verifies existing safety posture.
items.reviewer_notes: Docs-only audit. Critical for safe public messaging.

items.title: Golden path failure diagnostics
items.reason: When smoke_reviewer_golden_path.py fails, the output should guide the reviewer to the specific failing check and suggest remediation steps.
items.acceptance_criteria: Golden path script prints structured diagnostics per step with suggested fixes. Exit code indicates which category failed (install, CLI, safety, etc.).
items.non_goals: Does not auto-fix failures. Does not change the commands being tested.
items.reviewer_notes: Improves reviewer onboarding experience.

items.title: Evidence bundle readability improvements
items.reason: The release evidence bundle produces JSON and Markdown. The Markdown report could be more scannable for reviewers.
items.acceptance_criteria: Evidence bundle Markdown includes a summary table at the top and clear pass/fail indicators for each check.
items.non_goals: Does not change what checks run. Does not add network calls.
items.reviewer_notes: Polish item. Does not affect correctness.

items.title: Product capability inventory docs clarity
items.reason: The capability inventory is new. Reviewers need clear guidance on how to read statuses and claim levels.
items.acceptance_criteria: docs/product-capability-inventory.md includes a 'How to read this document' section and links to the gap prioritization plan.
items.non_goals: Does not change capability statuses. Does not add new capabilities.
items.reviewer_notes: Docs-only. Ensures the inventory is actually useful to reviewers.

items.title: Default live trading enablement
items.reason: Live trading must remain disabled by default. Any change that would enable live trading without explicit multi-factor opt-in contradicts the safety posture.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A — this capability must never be built.
items.non_goals: N/A
items.reviewer_notes: Explicitly out of scope. Safety boundary.

items.title: Profitability or trading signal performance claims
items.reason: Atlas must never claim profitability, guarantee returns, or evaluate trading signal quality. This is a legal and safety boundary.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A — this capability must never be built.
items.non_goals: N/A
items.reviewer_notes: Explicitly out of scope. Forbidden by project policy.

items.title: Safety-gate bypass features
items.reason: No feature may bypass the kill switch, approval queue, risk manager, or opt-in gates. Bypass features would destroy the safety model.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A — this capability must never be built.
items.non_goals: N/A
items.reviewer_notes: Explicitly out of scope. Core safety boundary.

items.title: Credential sharing flows
items.reason: Atlas must never facilitate sharing broker credentials, API keys, or account details between users or with the project maintainers. This is a security boundary and out of scope.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A — this capability must never be built.
items.non_goals: N/A
items.reviewer_notes: Explicitly out of scope. Security boundary.

items.title: Autonomous real-money execution
items.reason: Atlas is a supervised workspace, not an autonomous trading bot. Unattended real-money execution is explicitly out of scope.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A — this capability must never be built.
items.non_goals: N/A
items.reviewer_notes: Explicitly out of scope. Project mission boundary.

items.title: Full provider execution unlock
items.reason: Provider execution is currently governed by artifact policy and risk manager. A runtime unlock would require extensive safety review and is not a v0.5.8 goal.
items.acceptance_criteria: Runtime provider execution is gated by the same 8+ conditions as live broker submit, with explicit opt-in, audit record, and kill-switch check.
items.non_goals: Does not enable provider execution by default. Does not bypass risk manager.
items.reviewer_notes: Defer until post-v0.5.8 with full safety review.

items.title: Live broker execution for non-Alpaca brokers
items.reason: Live broker execution is blocked by default for all brokers. Expanding beyond Alpaca requires adapter completion and safety review.
items.acceptance_criteria: Broker adapter implements full Broker interface, passes live sync validation, and is gated by BrokerResolver.can_submit.
items.non_goals: Does not change can_submit default. Does not enable live submit without opt-in.
items.reviewer_notes: Defer until post-v0.5.8. Alpaca is sufficient for v0.5.7/v0.5.8.

items.title: Advanced dashboard features
items.reason: Dashboard is functional static HTML. Real-time streaming, interactive charts, or multi-user views are not v0.5.8 blockers.
items.acceptance_criteria: Dashboard renders static HTML with current system state. No advanced features required for v0.5.8.
items.non_goals: Does not add WebSocket streaming. Does not add authentication. Does not expose secrets.
items.reviewer_notes: Current dashboard is sufficient for v0.5.8.

items.title: Multi-user or cloud deployment
items.reason: Atlas is designed as a single-user local-first tool. Multi-user or cloud deployment would require significant architectural changes.
items.acceptance_criteria: N/A for v0.5.8. Local-first single-user model remains.
items.non_goals: Does not add user management. Does not add cloud persistence. Does not change the local-first design.
items.reviewer_notes: Out of scope for v0.5.8. May be reconsidered for a future major version.

items.title: Checker gap: ensure capability inventory stays current
items.reason: As capabilities change, the inventory must stay accurate. The existing checker verifies structure but does not detect stale claims.
items.acceptance_criteria: check_product_capability_inventory.py verifies that all capabilities marked safe_to_claim have corresponding CLI commands or docs that exist in the repo.
items.non_goals: Does not auto-update the inventory. Only detects structural drift.
items.reviewer_notes: Improves inventory integrity over time.

items.title: CLI UX clarity: help text for research command families
items.reason: The research command family has 174 commands. New users may find it hard to discover the right command.
items.acceptance_criteria: atlas research --help includes a short description of each command family (plan, dry-run, state, audit, etc.).
items.non_goals: Does not remove commands. Does not change command names.
items.reviewer_notes: Polish item. Improves discoverability.
